plugin_desc: SNR (NAG) switch-specific SNMP collection (NAG-MIB, IANA PEN 40418). In addition to standard IF-MIB interface metrics, it collects SNR private OIDs for device health: per-slot CPU utilization (sysCpuUsage), system memory (total/used), chassis temperature (sysTemperature, dedicated typed column) and fan state (sysFanStatus). Fan raw codes (0=Normal, 1=Abnormal) are normalized via telegraf processors.enum to 1=healthy / 2=fault. Memory utilization is computed from used/total. NAG-MIB exposes no dedicated power-supply (PSU) state object, so PSU is not collected (N/A). Supported brands/models — SNR (NAG) managed switches.
name: Switch

metrics.name: device_cpu_usage
metrics.description: This metric reports the CPU utilization of the SNR (NAG) device control plane (NAG-MIB sysCpuUsage), averaged across slots and normalized to 0-100. Sustained high CPU usage can indicate control-plane overload, routing instability, or attack traffic, and may lead to slow management response or packet processing delays.

metrics.name: device_memory_total
metrics.description: This metric reports the total size in bytes of the SNR (NAG) system memory (NAG-MIB sysMemorySize). Together with the used bytes it determines overall memory utilization.

metrics.name: device_memory_used
metrics.description: This metric reports the number of bytes currently in use by the SNR (NAG) system memory (NAG-MIB sysMemoryBusy). It helps administrators understand absolute memory consumption and identify when the device is under memory pressure.

metrics.name: device_memory_usage
metrics.description: This metric reports overall memory utilization of the SNR (NAG) device, computed as used / total * 100. High memory usage may indicate memory leaks, oversized routing/MAC/ARP tables, or insufficient capacity, and can cause instability or failed allocations.

metrics.name: device_temperature_celsius
metrics.description: This metric reports the chassis temperature in degrees Celsius of the SNR (NAG) device (NAG-MIB sysTemperature, a dedicated typed temperature column), aggregated as the maximum across slots. High temperatures can indicate cooling failure or airflow obstruction and risk thermal shutdown.

metrics.name: device_fan_state
metrics.description: This metric reports the fan state of the SNR (NAG) device (NAG-MIB sysFanStatus, raw 0=Normal / 1=Abnormal), normalized via telegraf processors.enum so that 1 = healthy and 2 = fault. Aggregated as the maximum across fans so any faulty fan surfaces. A value greater than 1 indicates a fan failure that can lead to overheating.

metrics.name: interface_ifHCInOctets
metrics.description: This metric indicates the average rate of bytes received over the past 5 minutes, measured in bytes per second, from the 64-bit IF-MIB ifHCInOctets counter (ifXTable). The 64-bit counter avoids the rapid wrap of the 32-bit ifInOctets on high-speed switch ports, keeping the rate accurate.

metrics.name: interface_ifHCOutOctets
metrics.description: This metric calculates the average rate of bytes sent over the past 5 minutes, measured in bytes per second, from the 64-bit IF-MIB ifHCOutOctets counter (ifXTable). The 64-bit counter avoids the rapid wrap of the 32-bit ifOutOctets on high-speed switch ports, keeping the rate accurate.

metrics.name: snmp_uptime
metrics.description: This metric indicates the uptime of the device since the last restart. By monitoring the uptime, network administrators can understand the stability and reliability of the device and identify potential reboot or failure issues.
